index.name: Nifty IT Index
index.description: Nifty IT tracks 10 companies generating $250B+ annual exports — 15% of India's total exports. Tier-1 majors: TCS (28.9%), Infosys (24.6%), HCL Tech (14.8%), Wipro (12.3%). India's IT sector accounts for 5.4M jobs, ~5% of India's GDP. Structural advantages: English-language competency, timezone arbitrage (US daytime = India nighttime), cost advantage (developer salary ₹40L vs $150K US), and global onsite-offshore model. Currency play: 60%+ revenues earned in USD; when rupee weakens 10%, reported rupee revenues rise equivalently. Bull cycle driver: Generative AI adoption in enterprise — IT spending growth accelerating from 6% to 12%+ in AI-driven consulting, platform modernization, and custom software development. Bear cycle driver: US tech spending contraction — most revenue from BFSI, telecom, and hi-tech verticals which curtail IT budgets during recessions. Leverage to market multiples: during risk-on sentiment, IT trades at 25-30x PE; during risk-off, contracts to 15-18x PE. Margin compression risk: wage inflation in India (30% CAGR for fresher salaries from 2010-2023) eroding profitability despite revenue growth.

holdings.name: TCS
holdings.description: India's largest IT company by revenue and market cap. Serves 1,200+ clients across 150 locations in 50+ countries. Market leader in BFSI, retail and manufacturing verticals. Generative AI deals ramping rapidly — over 100 AI-driven large deal wins announced in FY25.

holdings.name: Infosys
holdings.description: Global IT pioneer with Cobalt cloud platform. Strong in consulting-led large deals — $4B+ TCV quarterly. Carbon-neutral operations since 2020. AI-first strategy with Infosys Topaz platform for enterprise AI transformation.

holdings.name: HCL Technologies
holdings.description: Products-led IT company with HCL Software generating $650M+ annual recurring revenue. IBM software IP portfolio acquisition a game-changer. Strong ER&D services in auto, aerospace and semiconductors.

holdings.name: Wipro
holdings.description: Diversified IT services transforming with consulting-led strategy. Capco (BFSI), Rizing (SAP), Edgile (Cyber) acquisitions elevate margins. 230,000+ employees across 65 countries. Wipro Enterprise AI platform launched.

holdings.name: Tech Mahindra
holdings.description: Telecom-focused IT company under Project Reboot transformation. 5G software and infrastructure a key differentiator. TechM Business Boost AI platform for enterprise clients. Diversifying into BFSI, healthcare and manufacturing.

holdings.name: LTIMindtree
holdings.description: Merged entity of L&T Infotech and Mindtree creating a $4B+ revenue IT major. Strong in BFSI, hi-tech, media and manufacturing. Canvas AI platform driving digital transformation deals across Fortune 500 clients.

holdings.name: L&T Technology Services
holdings.description: Pure-play engineering R&D services company. Strong in automotive EV (35% revenue), industrial, telecom and medical devices verticals. Beneficiary of global ER&D spend rising to $2.4T by 2030.

holdings.name: Mphasis
holdings.description: Deep BFSI-focused IT company with DXC Technology lineage. AI-first with DeepInsights platform. Strong US mortgage and banking sector exposure. CEO Nitin Rakesh driving hyper-personalization and cloud-first mandate.

holdings.name: Persistent Systems
holdings.description: Fastest-growing large mid-cap IT company. Specializes in product engineering, Salesforce, and cloud-native development. Salesforce Partner of the Year multiple times. 25%+ revenue growth for 8+ consecutive quarters.

holdings.name: Coforge
holdings.description: Formerly NIIT Technologies. Transformed into a high-growth IT services company. Deep vertical expertise in travel, BFSI and insurance. Xceltrait and SLK acquisition expanding US market. Strong deal momentum at $1B+ TCV.

index.name: Nifty IT Index
index.description: Nippon India ETF IT tracks the Nifty IT Index  India's 10 largest technology services companies. India's IT sector is the world's largest IT services outsourcing ecosystem, with $254B in FY25 revenues and targeting $500B by 2030. The sector benefits from structural demand: AI transformation projects (cloud migration, GenAI implementation, enterprise automation) require massive workforce that only India can supply competitively. Wage arbitrage persists  Indian engineers cost 20-25% of US equivalents for same productivity. Key investment thesis: every $1 of AI infrastructure investment by Big Tech requires $10-15 of AI implementation services  the primary beneficiary is Indian IT services. Cyclical risk: US/Europe IT spending cuts during recession compress margins. However, discretionary project deferral is offset by non-discretionary managed services contracts (40-60% of revenues) providing floor revenues.

holdings.name: Tata Consultancy Services
holdings.description: India's largest software exporter  $29B revenues, 600,000+ employees, 55+ countries. GenAI platform WisdomNext and AI.Cloud driving next growth cycle. Tata Group ownership ensures conservative capital allocation and governance. Best-in-class attrition, client retention and deal conversion metrics.

holdings.name: Infosys
holdings.description: India's 2nd largest tech exporter  $19B revenue, 320,000 employees. Cobalt cloud migration platform and Topaz generative AI platform leading the AI transformation narrative. US banking and financial services vertical at $5B+ revenue. Consistent industry-leading revenue growth since CEO Salil Parekh's tenure.

holdings.name: HCL Technologies
holdings.description: Strongest growth among large-cap IT for the past 3 years. HCL Software (IBM partnership  acquired 9 enterprise software products) creates high-margin, recurring SaaS revenues. ER&D (Engineering R&D) services growing 20%+ serving semiconductor, auto and aerospace clients.

holdings.name: Wipro
holdings.description: Global tech services and consulting firm. Capco (UK financial consulting) and Rizing (SAP consulting) acquisitions expanding consulting margins. Azim Premji's philanthropy ($21B donated) makes Wipro an ESG darling. Defence IT and government IT growing via GovEdge platform.

holdings.name: LTIMindtree
holdings.description: Post-merger of L&T Infotech and Mindtree  India's 5th largest IT company. AI-first Canvas platform for intelligent automation. Strong in financial services and manufacturing verticals. L&T Group backing provides stable deal pipeline from the conglomerate's project ecosystem.

holdings.name: Tech Mahindra
holdings.description: Leading telecom IT services provider globally  partners with 150+ telecom operators for 5G BSS/OSS systems. M&M Group backing. Engineering Services (Comviva, BFL Software) and BPS (Hinduja Global Solutions) adding diversification. CEO Mohit Joshi driving Project Fortius for margin recovery.

holdings.name: Infosys
holdings.description: India's 2nd largest IT firm. Infosys Topaz AI platform enables clients to build enterprise AI applications. Large deal wins in cloud migration and core banking transformation. 30-year record of dividend payments, regular buybacks.

holdings.name: HCL Technologies
holdings.description: 3rd largest with unique Products & Platforms (HCL Software) segment. ERPNext, DRYiCE AIOps, Calpont analytics — recurring SaaS revenue. Engineering R&D services (Volvo, BMW, Airbus) growing with EV and autonomous vehicle tech spending.

holdings.name: Wipro
holdings.description: India's 4th largest IT. Under Thierry Delaporte's restructuring — customer-centric model, fewer verticals. Capco acquisition (fintech consulting) and Edgile (cybersecurity) add high-margin consulting revenue. EBITDA margins recovering.

holdings.name: Tech Mahindra
holdings.description: 5G telecom IT specialist — network virtualisation (5G CNF/VNF), OSS/BSS modernisation for AT&T, Comcast, Telstra. Also strong in engineering (aerospace, auto). 'Rise with Tech' transformation under Mohit Joshi rebuilding revenue growth.

holdings.name: LTIMindtree
holdings.description: L&T's marquee IT company formed by LTI + Mindtree merger. Strong in BFSI (especially capital markets), travel & hospitality (Amadeus, Sabre integration) and manufacturing. Canvas platform accelerates cloud and data modernisation for large enterprises.
